calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

outcome.source_quote: In 2023, the WRRT delivered decisive results. The team carried out more than 613 operations, rescuing over 1,345 live animals and seizing approximately 601 kg of illegal wildlife meat.

source.quote: In 2023, the WRRT delivered decisive results. The team carried out more than 613 operations, rescuing over 1,345 live animals and seizing approximately 601 kg of illegal wildlife meat.

outcome.source_quote: In 2023, PTWRC received 1,302 animals through confiscation and donation.

source.quote: In 2023, PTWRC received 1,302 animals through confiscation and donation.

outcome.source_quote: while 753 animals, including pythons and macaques, were successfully released back into the wild.

source.quote: while 753 animals, including pythons and macaques, were successfully released back into the wild.

outcome.source_quote: More than 534 wild animals were rescued, many of them victims of poaching or trafficking.

source.quote: More than 534 wild animals were rescued, many of them victims of poaching or trafficking.

outcome.source_quote: At the same time, 103 animals were born, showcasing effective care and captive breeding efforts

source.quote: At the same time, 103 animals were born, showcasing effective care and captive breeding efforts
while 30 animals were successfully released.

outcome.source_quote: Seventeen young long-tailed macaques were integrated into existing troops.

source.quote: Seventeen young long-tailed macaques were integrated into existing troops.

outcome.source_quote: joined by ten wild boars in November, strengthening the prey base for future tigers.

source.quote: joined by ten wild boars in November, strengthening the prey base for future tigers.

outcome.source_quote: We released seven animals this year, including a wreathed hornbill, a pair of hand-raised wreathed hornbills, a rescued water monitor lizard, a bottle-raised leopard cat, and two common palm civets.

source.quote: We released seven animals this year, including a wreathed hornbill, a pair of hand-raised wreathed hornbills, a rescued water monitor lizard, a bottle-raised leopard cat, and two common palm civets.

outcome.source_quote: Wildlife Alliance transferred four sambar deer to the Tiger Reintroduction Station in July

source.quote: Wildlife Alliance transferred four sambar deer to the Tiger Reintroduction Station in July

outcome.source_quote: 43 animals arrived, including pangolins and macaques, with three pangolins born (of whom two survived).

source.quote: 43 animals arrived, including pangolins and macaques, with three pangolins born (of whom two survived).

calculated_metrics.name: Impact Equivalency Score (IES)

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: No valid counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.
Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Overall, 435 animals died due to prior mistreatment or severe stress before arrival.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: In 2023, the WRRT delivered decisive results. The team carried out more than 613 operations, rescuing over 1,345 live animals and seizing approximately 601 kg of illegal wildlife meat.

beneficiaries.location: Phnom Tamao Wildlife Rescue Centre, Cambodia

source.quote: At the same time, 103 animals were born, showcasing effective care and captive breeding efforts

beneficiaries.location: Wildlife Release Station (WRS) in Koh Kong, Cambodia

source.quote: In 2023, 43 animals arrived, including pangolins and macaques, with three pangolins born (of whom two survived).

beneficiaries.location: Wildlife Release Station (WRS) in Koh Kong, Cambodia

source.quote: In 2023, 43 animals arrived, including pangolins and macaques, with three pangolins born (of whom two survived).
We released seven animals this year, including a wreathed hornbill, a pair of hand-raised wreathed hornbills, a rescued water monitor lizard, a bottle-raised leopard cat, and two common palm civets.
WILDLIFE ALLIANCE Direct Protection to Forests & Wildlife

metrics.context_qualifier: by Wildlife Rapid Rescue Team (WRRT) operations

source.quote: In 2023, the WRRT delivered decisive results. The team carried out more than 613 operations, rescuing over 1,345 live animals and seizing approximately 601 kg of illegal wildlife meat.

metrics.context_qualifier: at Phnom Tamao Wildlife Rescue Centre (PTWRC) through confiscation and donation

source.quote: In 2023, PTWRC received 1,302 animals through confiscation and donation.

metrics.context_qualifier: from Phnom Tamao Wildlife Rescue Centre (PTWRC) back into the wild

source.quote: while 753 animals, including pythons and macaques, were successfully released back into the wild.

metrics.context_qualifier: from poaching or trafficking in Cardamom Rainforest Landscape

source.quote: More than 534 wild animals were rescued, many of them victims of poaching or trafficking.
44,675 Snares removed

metrics.context_qualifier: by Community Anti-Poaching Units (CAPU) surrounding Wildlife Release Station (WRS)

source.quote: The Community Anti-Poaching Units (CAPU) split into two teams that patrolled the forest surrounding WRS, removing 1,011 snares for larger animals, civets, and three birds and macaques nets.
rescuing over 1,345 live animals and seizing approximately 601 kg of illegal wildlife meat.

metrics.context_qualifier: at Phnom Tamao Wildlife Rescue Centre (PTWRC)

source.quote: At the same time, 103 animals were born, showcasing effective care and captive breeding efforts
while 30 animals were successfully released.

metrics.context_qualifier: into existing troops from Wildlife Release Station (WRS)

source.quote: Seventeen young long-tailed macaques were integrated into existing troops.

metrics.context_qualifier: from Angkor Wildlife Release project (including 10 otter pups and 2 gibbon babies)

source.quote: From January 1, 2023, to January 31, 2024, the project recorded 12 wildlife births, including ten otter pups and two gibbon babies.

metrics.context_qualifier: to Tiger Reintroduction Station to strengthen prey base

source.quote: joined by ten wild boars in November, strengthening the prey base for future tigers.
We released seven animals this year, including a wreathed hornbill, a pair of hand-raised wreathed hornbills, a rescued water monitor lizard, a bottle-raised leopard cat, and two common palm civets.

metrics.context_qualifier: to Tiger Reintroduction Station to strengthen prey base

source.quote: Wildlife Alliance transferred four sambar deer to the Tiger Reintroduction Station in July
43 animals arrived, including pangolins and macaques, with three pangolins born (of whom two survived).
Meta, a hand-raised sambar fawn, was released in December 2023
Overall, 435 animals died due to prior mistreatment or severe stress before arrival.
